original_abstract: Transformers have achieved superior performances in many tasks in natural language processing and computer vision, which also triggered great interest in the time series community. Among multiple advantages of Transformers, the ability to capture long-range dependencies and interactions is especially attractive for time series modeling, leading to exciting progress in various time series applications. In this paper, we systematically review Transformer schemes for time series modeling by highlighting their strengths as well as limitations. In particular, we examine the development of time series Transformers in two perspectives. From the perspective of network structure, we summarize the adaptations and modifications that have been made to Transformers in order to accommodate the challenges in time series analysis. From the perspective of applications, we categorize time series Transformers based on common tasks including forecasting, anomaly detection, and classification. Empirically, we perform robust analysis, model size analysis, and seasonal-trend decomposition analysis to study how Transformers perform in time series. Finally, we discuss and suggest future directions to provide useful research guidance.
humanized_abstract: A rapidly evolving research area, Transformers have achieved impressive success in many NLP and CV tasks. This has not only raised public awareness of Transformers, but also sparked strong interest in the time series community. While other benefits of using Transformers have been highlighted in many works, the ability of Transformers to model long-range dependency and relationships in data has been largely responsible for the recent breakthroughs in time series modeling and analysis, which makes this area an exciting one. This survey paper aims to provide a comprehensive overview of the properties and impacts of using Transformers for time series modeling. We investigate the development of time series Transformers from two viewpoints: (i) structural view from the network perspective, where we summarize the necessary modifications and operations on original Transformers to address the characteristics of time series data; and (ii) application view from various use cases such as forecasting, anomaly detection, and classification. We carry out the robustness analysis and the model size analysis to evaluate the performances of the proposed time series Transformers, as well as the trend analysis for seasonality decomposition. The insights from the analyses are discussed, and some possible future research ways are given to guide the community.
